title: viola purpurea subsp. aurea
intro: Viola purpurea subsp. aurea, also known as golden violet, is a species of flowering plant in the violet family (Violaceae). It is a perennial plant that grows to a height of 10-30 cm, with heart-shaped leaves and yellow-gold flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and saponins. The golden violet is used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and tinctures, which can help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, anxiety, and insomnia, promoting overall health and well-being.
